sanskrit: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च । तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
hindiTranslation: क्योंकि पैदा हुएकी जरूर मृत्यु होगी और मरे हुएका जरूर जन्म होगा ।  इस जन्ममरणरूप परिवर्तन के प्रवाह का परिहार अर्थात् निवारण नहीं हो सकता ।  अतः इस विषयमें तुम्हें शोक नहीं करना चाहिये ॥
englishTranslation: For what is born, death is certain; for what dies, birth is certain. What cannot be avoided should not be grieved over.
essence: What must change is not a reason for grief.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Anything that is born will die, and anything that dies will be born again. Since this cycle cannot be stopped, there is no reason to grieve over it.
simpleInsight: Some things are not yours to control, and peace begins where resistance ends.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen as Krishna keeps answering his grief. After saying the true self is beyond change, Krishna now grounds the lesson in the body’s cycle: birth, death, and return. The point is to stop mourning what nature itself keeps moving.
modernRelevance: You are sitting beside a hospital bed, watching a monitor you cannot negotiate with. The fear is real, but the fact is plain: some changes cannot be prevented, only met with steadiness.
translationLiteral: जातस्य (of the born) / हि (indeed) / ध्रुवः (certain) / मृत्युः (death) / ध्रुवम् (certain) / जन्म (birth) / मृतस्य (of the dead) / च (and) / तस्मात् (therefore) / अपरिहार्ये (in the unavoidable) / अर्थे (matter) / न (not) / त्वम् (you) / शोचितुम् (to grieve) / अर्हसि (should)
